sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Let me try pressing ACTION7 when the player is at rows 24-29
# Current: Player at rows 24-29, Blue at rows 13-16, Path at rows 53-53 (disconnected)

result = action(['ACTION7'])
print("ACTION7 result:", result)

seg = current_frame.segmentation
print("\nBlocks after ACTION7:")
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] in ['R', 'N', 'b']:
        b = n['boundary']
        minr = min(x[0] for x in b)
        minc = min(x[1] for x in b)
        maxr = max(x[0] for x in b)
        maxc = max(x[1] for x in b)
        if minr < 54:
            print(f"  {n['color']}: rows {minr}-{maxr}, cols {minc}-{maxc}")

for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'M' and n['pixels'] >= 18:
        b = n['boundary']
        minr = min(x[0] for x in b)
        maxr = max(x[0] for x in b)
        print(f"Player: rows {minr}-{maxr}")
        break

# Check path
print("\nPath info:")
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'g' and n['pixels'] > 10:
        b = n['boundary']
        minr = min(x[0] for x in b)
        minc = min(x[1] for x in b)
        maxr = max(x[0] for x in b)
        maxc = max(x[1] for x in b)
        if minr < 54:
            print(f"  g path: rows {minr}-{maxr}, cols {minc}-{maxc}, pixels={n['pixels']}")

print("\nLevel completed:", result.get('level_completed'))
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
